description.en: The American bison is the heaviest land animal in North America, known for its massive head and shaggy coat.
description.zh: 美洲野牛是北美洲最重的陆地动物，以其巨大的头部和蓬松的皮毛而闻名。

anatomy.en: The American bison is a massive animal that can weigh up to 900 kilograms and stand about 1.8 meters tall at the shoulder. It has a huge, shaggy head with short, curved horns and a large muscular hump on its back. Its thick, dark brown fur keeps it warm during freezing winters on the plains.
anatomy.zh: 美洲野牛是一种巨大的动物，体重可达900公斤，肩高约1.8米。它有一个巨大而毛茸茸的头，长着短而弯曲的角，背上有一个大大的肌肉隆起。它厚厚的深棕色皮毛可以帮助它在平原的寒冬中保暖。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bison are herbivores that spend most of their day grazing on grasses and sedges. They live in large herds and can run surprisingly fast, reaching speeds of up to 55 kilometers per hour. During mating season in summer, male bison charge and headbutt each other to compete for mates.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 野牛是草食动物，一天中大部分时间都在吃草和莎草。它们成群结队地生活，奔跑速度非常快，最快可达每小时55公里。在夏天的繁殖季节，雄性野牛会互相冲撞和顶头来争夺伴侣。

habitat_and_distribution.en: American bison once roamed the Great Plains of North America in herds of millions. Today they live mainly in national parks and protected grasslands in the United States and Canada. Yellowstone National Park is one of the most famous places where wild bison still roam freely.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲野牛曾经以数百万头的规模在北美大平原上漫游。如今它们主要生活在美国和加拿大的国家公园和受保护的草原上。黄石国家公园是最著名的仍有野牛自由漫步的地方之一。

habitat.text_en: Great Plains
habitat.text_zh: 大平原

description.en: Brown bears are large, powerful predators distributed across North America, Europe, and Asia.
description.zh: 棕熊是大型且强壮的捕食者，广泛分布于北美洲、欧洲和亚洲。

anatomy.en: Brown bears are among the largest land predators on Earth, with males weighing up to 600 kilograms. They have a distinctive muscular hump on their shoulders that gives them powerful digging strength. Their fur color ranges from blonde to dark brown, and they have long, curved claws that can be over 10 centimeters long.
anatomy.zh: 棕熊是地球上最大的陆地捕食者之一，雄性体重可达600公斤。它们的肩膀上有一个独特的肌肉隆起，这给了它们强大的挖掘力量。它们的毛色从金黄色到深棕色不等，弯曲的爪子可以超过10厘米长。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Brown bears are omnivores that eat berries, roots, fish, and small mammals. They are famous for catching salmon as the fish leap up waterfalls during spawning season. In winter, brown bears hibernate in dens for several months, living off the fat they stored during autumn.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 棕熊是杂食动物，吃浆果、根茎、鱼和小型哺乳动物。它们最出名的就是在鲑鱼产卵季节，在瀑布旁抓住跳跃的鲑鱼。冬天时，棕熊会在洞穴中冬眠好几个月，靠秋天储存的脂肪维生。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Brown bears live in forests, mountains, and river valleys across North America, Europe, and Asia. In North America, they are commonly found in Alaska and western Canada. The grizzly bear, a well-known subspecies, lives in the Rocky Mountains and Pacific Northwest.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 棕熊生活在北美洲、欧洲和亚洲的森林、山脉和河谷地带。在北美洲，它们常见于阿拉斯加和加拿大西部。灰熊是一种著名的亚种，生活在落基山脉和太平洋西北地区。

habitat.text_en: Forests and mountainous regions
habitat.text_zh: 森林与山区

description.en: Also known as cougars or pumas, these large, secretive cats are highly adaptable predators.
description.zh: 也被称为美洲豹，这些大型且隐秘的猫科动物是适应性极强的捕食者。

anatomy.en: Mountain lions are sleek, muscular cats that can weigh up to 100 kilograms and measure over 2 meters from nose to tail. They have tawny, golden-brown fur, small rounded ears, and a long, heavy tail that helps them balance while running and jumping. Their powerful hind legs allow them to leap as far as 12 meters in a single bound.
anatomy.zh: 美洲狮是体型光滑而肌肉发达的猫科动物，体重可达100公斤，从鼻子到尾巴长度超过2米。它们有着黄褐色的金棕色毛皮、小而圆的耳朵和一条长而粗的尾巴，帮助它们在奔跑和跳跃时保持平衡。强壮的后腿让它们可以一次跳远达12米。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Mountain lions are solitary hunters that stalk and ambush their prey, mainly deer. They are most active at dawn and dusk and can roam territories as large as 800 square kilometers. After a successful hunt, a mountain lion may hide its food under leaves and return to eat over several days.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 美洲狮是独居的猎手，会悄悄跟踪并伏击猎物，主要捕食鹿。它们在黎明和黄昏时最活跃，活动范围可达800平方公里。一次成功捕猎后，美洲狮会把食物藏在树叶下面，然后连续好几天回来吃。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Mountain lions have the widest range of any wild cat in the Americas, stretching from Canada to southern Chile. They live in mountains, deserts, forests, and even swamps. In the United States, they are most common in the western states.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲狮的分布范围是美洲所有野生猫科动物中最广的，从加拿大一直延伸到南美洲的智利南部。它们栖息在山脉、沙漠、森林甚至沼泽地带。在美国，它们在西部各州最为常见。

habitat.text_en: Mountains and deserts
habitat.text_zh: 山脉与沙漠

description.en: Sea otters are marine mammals that use tools like rocks to crack open shells while floating on their backs.
description.zh: 海獭是海洋哺乳动物，它们在仰泳时会使用石头等工具敲开贝壳。

anatomy.en: Sea otters are the smallest marine mammals, weighing between 14 and 45 kilograms. They have the thickest fur of any animal, with up to 150,000 hairs per square centimeter, which keeps them warm in cold ocean water. Their webbed hind feet act like flippers, and their sensitive front paws are perfect for grabbing food.
anatomy.zh: 海獭是最小的海洋哺乳动物，体重在14到45公斤之间。它们拥有所有动物中最浓密的毛发，每平方厘米多达15万根毛，可以在冰冷的海水中保暖。它们有蹼的后脚像鳍一样好用，灵敏的前爪非常适合抓取食物。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Sea otters eat sea urchins, crabs, clams, and other shellfish, using rocks as tools to crack them open on their bellies. They often float together in groups called rafts and even hold paws while sleeping so they do not drift apart. By eating sea urchins, sea otters help protect kelp forests, which are important ocean habitats.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 海獭吃海胆、螃蟹、蛤蜊和其他贝类，会用石头当工具在肚子上把壳敲开。它们经常成群地一起漂浮，这叫做「筏群」，睡觉时还会手牵手，以免彼此漂散。通过吃海胆，海獭帮助保护了海藻森林，这是非常重要的海洋栖息地。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Sea otters live along the coasts of the northern Pacific Ocean, from California to Alaska, and across to Russia and Japan. They spend almost their entire lives in the water, preferring shallow coastal areas with kelp forests. Historically they were hunted nearly to extinction for their fur, and today their populations are slowly recovering.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 海獭生活在北太平洋沿岸，从加利福尼亚到阿拉斯加，一直到俄罗斯和日本。它们几乎整个一生都在水里度过，喜欢有海藻森林的浅海沿岸地区。历史上它们曾因毛皮被猎杀到几乎灭绝，如今数量正在慢慢恢复。

habitat.text_en: Coastal Pacific Ocean
habitat.text_zh: 太平洋沿岸

description.en: Raccoons are highly intelligent mammals, easily recognized by their bandit-like black face masks.
description.zh: 浣熊是高度聪明的哺乳动物，很容易通过它们像强盗一样的黑色面罩认出来。

anatomy.en: Raccoons are medium-sized mammals weighing between 5 and 12 kilograms. Their most famous feature is the black mask across their eyes and a bushy, ringed tail with alternating dark and light bands. They have extremely nimble front paws with five fingers, almost like tiny hands, which they use to open jars, doors, and even latches.
anatomy.zh: 浣熊是中等体型的哺乳动物，体重在5到12公斤之间。它们最出名的特征是眼睛上的黑色面罩和一条蓬松的环纹尾巴，深浅条纹交替排列。它们的前爪有五根手指，非常灵巧，几乎像小手一样，可以打开罐子、门，甚至门闩。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Raccoons are omnivores that eat almost anything, including fruits, insects, eggs, fish, and even garbage. They are mostly active at night and are famous for washing their food in water before eating it. Raccoons are very clever problem-solvers and can remember solutions to tasks for up to three years.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 浣熊是杂食动物，几乎什么都吃，包括水果、昆虫、蛋、鱼，甚至垃圾。它们大多在夜间活动，最出名的就是在吃东西之前把食物在水里洗一洗。浣熊非常聪明，善于解决问题，能记住解决方法长达三年之久。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Raccoons originally lived in the forests of North America, but today they have spread to cities, suburbs, and even parts of Europe and Japan. They are very adaptable and can make their homes in tree hollows, attics, sewers, or abandoned burrows. They are most common in the eastern and central United States.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 浣熊最初生活在北美洲的森林里，但如今已经扩展到了城市、郊区，甚至欧洲和日本的一些地方。它们适应性很强，可以在树洞、阁楼、下水道或废弃的洞穴中安家。它们在美国东部和中部最为常见。

habitat.text_en: Woodlands and urban areas
habitat.text_zh: 林地和城市地区

description.en: The moose is the largest member of the deer family, famous for the enormous, flat antlers of the males.
description.zh: 驼鹿是鹿科动物中体型最大的，以雄性巨大的扁平鹿角而闻名。

anatomy.en: Moose are the tallest members of the deer family, standing up to 2.1 meters at the shoulder and weighing up to 700 kilograms. Male moose grow enormous palmate antlers that can span 1.8 meters wide and are shed and regrown every year. They have long legs, a drooping nose, and a flap of skin called a dewlap hanging under the chin.
anatomy.zh: 驼鹿是鹿科动物中最高的，肩高可达2.1米，体重可达700公斤。雄性驼鹿长着巨大的掌状鹿角，宽度可达1.8米，每年脱落后重新长出来。它们有长长的腿、下垂的鼻子，下巴下面还挂着一块叫做「肉垂」的皮肤。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Moose are herbivores that feed on leaves, bark, twigs, and aquatic plants. They are excellent swimmers and can dive underwater to eat water plants from the bottom of lakes. Despite their calm appearance, moose can be very aggressive, especially mothers protecting their calves.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 驼鹿是草食动物，吃树叶、树皮、嫩枝和水生植物。它们是出色的游泳者，可以潜入水下吃湖底的水草。尽管驼鹿看起来很温和，但它们其实可能非常凶猛，尤其是保护小鹿的母驼鹿。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Moose live in boreal and mixed forests across North America, Europe, and Asia. In North America, they are found in Alaska, Canada, and the northern United States. They prefer areas near lakes, ponds, and wetlands where they can cool off and find aquatic food.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 驼鹿生活在北美洲、欧洲和亚洲的北方针叶林和混交林中。在北美洲，它们分布在阿拉斯加、加拿大和美国北部。它们喜欢靠近湖泊、池塘和湿地的地方，可以在那里乘凉和寻找水生食物。

habitat.text_en: Boreal and mixed deciduous forests
habitat.text_zh: 北方针叶林和混交林

description.en: Coyotes are highly vocal and adaptable canines that can thrive in deserts, forests, and even cities.
description.zh: 郊狼是发声频繁且适应性极强的犬科动物，能在沙漠、森林甚至城市中繁衍。

anatomy.en: Coyotes are medium-sized canines that look like a smaller, thinner version of a wolf. They weigh between 7 and 20 kilograms and have pointed ears, a narrow snout, and a bushy tail with a black tip. Their fur is usually grayish-brown on top and lighter on the belly, helping them blend into their surroundings.
anatomy.zh: 郊狼是中等体型的犬科动物，看起来像是狼的缩小版，更加纤细。它们体重在7到20公斤之间，有尖尖的耳朵、细长的鼻子和一条末端为黑色的蓬松尾巴。它们的毛通常上面是灰棕色、腹部较浅，有助于融入周围环境。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Coyotes are omnivores that eat rabbits, rodents, fruit, insects, and sometimes garbage from humans. They are famous for their haunting howls, which they use to communicate with family members across long distances. Coyotes often hunt alone or in pairs, but may form small packs in winter when larger prey is available.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 郊狼是杂食动物，吃兔子、啮齿类动物、水果、昆虫，有时还吃人类的垃圾。它们最出名的是那令人难忘的嚎叫声，用来和远方的家人沟通。郊狼通常单独或成对狩猎，但在冬天有较大猎物时，可能会组成小群体。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Coyotes originally lived in the prairies and deserts of western North America, but have now spread to almost every corner of the continent, including big cities like Los Angeles and Chicago. They are one of the most adaptable animals in the world. They can live in deserts, grasslands, forests, mountains, and urban neighborhoods.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 郊狼最初生活在北美洲西部的草原和沙漠中，但如今已经扩展到了大陆的几乎每一个角落，包括洛杉矶和芝加哥等大城市。它们是世界上适应性最强的动物之一，能生活在沙漠、草原、森林、山脉和城市社区中。

habitat.text_en: Diverse regions across North America
habitat.text_zh: 北美洲的各个地区

description.en: The only marsupial found in North America, known for 'playing dead' when threatened.
description.zh: 北美洲唯一的有袋类动物，以在受到威胁时「装死」而闻名。

anatomy.en: Virginia opossums are about the size of a house cat, weighing between 2 and 6 kilograms. They have grayish-white fur, a pointed pink nose, small dark eyes, and a long, hairless tail that can grip branches like an extra hand. Females carry their tiny babies in a pouch on their belly, just like kangaroos.
anatomy.zh: 北美负鼠大约和家猫一样大，体重在2到6公斤之间。它们有灰白色的毛、粉红色的尖鼻子、黑色的小眼睛，还有一条长长的无毛尾巴，可以像多一只手一样抓住树枝。母负鼠把她的小宝宝放在肚子上的袋子里，就像袋鼠一样。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Opossums are omnivores that eat insects, fruit, small animals, and even ticks — a single opossum can eat thousands of ticks in a season, helping control disease. Their most famous trick is playing dead when they feel scared, rolling over with their tongue hanging out until the danger passes. They are nocturnal animals that are mostly active at night.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 负鼠是杂食动物，吃昆虫、水果、小动物，甚至蜱虫——一只负鼠一个季节可以吃掉几千只蜱虫，帮助控制疾病传播。它们最出名的绝招就是在害怕时装死，翻倒在地上伸出舌头，直到危险过去。它们是夜行动物，大多在夜间活动。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Virginia opossums live in woodlands, thickets, farmlands, and suburban areas throughout the eastern United States, Mexico, and Central America. They are very adaptable and often make their homes in tree hollows, rock crevices, or under buildings. Their range has been slowly expanding northward due to milder winters.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 北美负鼠生活在美国东部、墨西哥和中美洲的林地、灌木丛、农田和郊区。它们适应性很强，经常在树洞、岩石缝隙或建筑物下面安家。由于冬天变暖，它们的分布范围正在慢慢向北扩展。

habitat.text_en: Woodlands and thickets
habitat.text_zh: 林地和灌木丛

description.en: Beavers are nature's engineers, famous for building dams and lodges in rivers and streams.
description.zh: 河狸是大自然的工程师，以在河流和溪流中建造水坝和巢穴而闻名。

anatomy.en: American beavers are the largest rodents in North America, weighing between 16 and 32 kilograms. They have a flat, paddle-shaped tail covered in scales that helps them swim and slap the water as a warning signal. Their large orange front teeth never stop growing and are strong enough to chew through tree trunks.
anatomy.zh: 美洲河狸是北美洲最大的啮齿动物，体重在16到32公斤之间。它们有一条扁平的、桨状的、覆盖着鳞片的尾巴，帮助它们游泳，还能拍打水面作为警告信号。它们大大的橙色门牙永远不会停止生长，而且强壮到可以啃断树干。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Beavers are herbivores that eat tree bark, leaves, roots, and water plants. They are famous for building dams out of sticks, mud, and stones, creating ponds that become homes for many other animals. Beaver families live together in dome-shaped lodges with underwater entrances that keep predators out.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 河狸是草食动物，吃树皮、树叶、根茎和水生植物。它们最出名的是用树枝、泥巴和石头建造水坝，创造出的池塘成为了许多其他动物的家园。河狸一家住在圆顶形的巢穴里，入口在水下，可以防止捕食者进入。

habitat_and_distribution.en: American beavers live in rivers, streams, ponds, and lakes across most of North America, from Canada to northern Mexico. They prefer areas with plenty of trees near water, which they need for both food and building materials. Their dam-building activities create wetlands that benefit many other species.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲河狸生活在北美洲大部分地区的河流、溪流、池塘和湖泊中，从加拿大到墨西哥北部都有分布。它们喜欢水边有很多树木的地方，因为树木既是食物又是建筑材料。它们建造水坝的活动创造了湿地，对许多其他物种都有益处。

habitat.text_en: Rivers, streams, and lakes
habitat.text_zh: 河流、溪流和湖泊

description.en: Named for their large, curved horns, these sheep are expert climbers in steep, rocky terrain.
description.zh: 因其巨大的弯曲羊角而得名，这些羊是陡峭岩石地形中的攀岩专家。

anatomy.en: Bighorn sheep weigh between 60 and 140 kilograms and are named for the males' massive curved horns, which can weigh up to 14 kilograms — as much as all the other bones in their body combined. Their hooves have a hard outer edge and a soft, grippy sole that works like a natural climbing shoe. Their brown fur with a white rump patch helps them blend into rocky mountainsides.
anatomy.zh: 大角羊体重在60到140公斤之间，因雄性巨大的弯曲角而得名，角的重量可达14公斤——和它们身体其他所有骨头加起来一样重。它们的蹄子外缘坚硬、底部柔软有抓地力，就像天然的攀岩鞋。棕色的毛皮和白色的臀部斑块帮助它们融入岩石山坡中。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bighorn sheep are herbivores that graze on grasses, shrubs, and other mountain plants. During the mating season, males run at each other at full speed and smash their horns together in dramatic head-butting battles that can be heard from far away. They live in groups and use their excellent vision to spot predators like mountain lions from a distance.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 大角羊是草食动物，吃草、灌木和其他山地植物。在交配季节，雄性会全速奔向对方，用角猛烈地互相碰撞，这种壮观的顶角战斗在很远的地方就能听到。它们群居生活，用极好的视力从远处发现美洲狮等捕食者。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Bighorn sheep live on steep, rocky mountain slopes and cliffs in western North America, from southern Canada to Mexico. They prefer open alpine meadows near cliffs where they can escape from predators by climbing to safety. Different subspecies live in the Rocky Mountains, the Sierra Nevada, and the desert mountains of the Southwest.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 大角羊生活在北美洲西部陡峭的岩石山坡和悬崖上，从加拿大南部到墨西哥都有分布。它们喜欢靠近悬崖的开阔高山草甸，可以通过攀爬到安全的地方躲避捕食者。不同的亚种分别生活在落基山脉、内华达山脉和西南部的沙漠山地。

habitat.text_en: Alpine meadows and steep cliffs
habitat.text_zh: 高山草甸和陡峭悬崖

description.en: A medium-sized wildcat with a distinctive short, 'bobbed' tail and tufted ears.
description.zh: 一种中等体型的野猫，具有独特的短尾巴和有簇毛的耳朵。

anatomy.en: The bobcat is about twice the size of a house cat, weighing 6 to 14 kilograms. It has long legs, large paws, and a short tail that looks like it was cut, which is how it got its name. Its fur is tan to grayish-brown with dark spots and stripes that help it blend into the forest.
anatomy.zh: 短尾猫大约是家猫的两倍大，体重在6到14公斤之间。它有长腿、大爪子和一条看起来像被剪断的短尾巴，这也是它名字的由来。它的毛皮呈棕褐色到灰褐色，带有深色斑点和条纹，帮助它融入森林之中。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bobcats are solitary hunters that mainly eat rabbits, hares, and small rodents. They are most active at dawn and dusk, sneaking up on prey before pouncing with a powerful leap. They mark their territory with scent and claw marks on trees.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 短尾猫是独居的猎手，主要捕食兔子、野兔和小型啮齿动物。它们在黎明和黄昏时最为活跃，悄悄靠近猎物后用有力的跳跃扑上去。它们用气味和在树上的爪痕来标记自己的领地。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Bobcats live in forests, swamps, deserts, and even near suburban areas across North America. They are found from southern Canada through most of the United States and into Mexico. They are one of the most adaptable wild cats in the world.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 短尾猫生活在北美洲的森林、沼泽、沙漠，甚至靠近郊区的地方。它们分布范围从加拿大南部到美国大部分地区，一直延伸到墨西哥。它们是世界上适应能力最强的野猫之一。

habitat.text_en: Forests, swamps, and deserts
habitat.text_zh: 森林、沼泽和沙漠

description.en: The pronghorn is the fastest land mammal in the Western Hemisphere, built for incredible speed.
description.zh: 叉角羚是西半球速度最快的陆地哺乳动物，天生为了惊人的速度而生。

anatomy.en: Pronghorns stand about 80 to 100 centimeters tall at the shoulder and weigh 40 to 65 kilograms. Both males and females have forked horns, but the male's horns are much larger. They have huge eyes for spotting danger far away and extra-large windpipes and lungs to take in lots of air while running.
anatomy.zh: 叉角羚肩高约80到100厘米，体重40到65公斤。雄性和雌性都有分叉的角，但雄性的角大得多。它们有很大的眼睛用来远距离发现危险，还有超大的气管和肺部，能在奔跑时吸入大量空气。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Pronghorns eat grasses, shrubs, and cacti. They can run at speeds up to 88 kilometers per hour, making them the second fastest land animal in the world after the cheetah. They live in herds and use their amazing eyesight to watch for predators like coyotes and wolves.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 叉角羚吃草、灌木和仙人掌。它们的奔跑速度可达每小时88公里，是世界上仅次于猎豹的第二快陆地动物。它们成群生活，用惊人的视力来观察郊狼和狼等捕食者。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Pronghorns live on the open plains and grasslands of western North America. They range from southern Canada through the western United States and into northern Mexico. They prefer wide, flat areas where they can see and outrun predators.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 叉角羚生活在北美洲西部的开阔平原和草原上。它们的分布范围从加拿大南部到美国西部，一直延伸到墨西哥北部。它们喜欢宽阔平坦的地方，这样就能看到并跑过捕食者。

habitat.text_en: Open plains and brushlands
habitat.text_zh: 开阔平原和灌木丛

description.en: Often called 'sea cows', these gentle giants slowly graze on underwater plants in warm coastal waters.
description.zh: 常被称为「海牛」，这些温和的庞然大物在温暖的沿海水域慢慢啃食水下植物。

anatomy.en: West Indian manatees are large, gray, barrel-shaped mammals that can grow up to 4 meters long and weigh over 500 kilograms. They have paddle-like flippers, a flat rounded tail, and small eyes. Their thick, wrinkled skin is often covered with algae, giving them a greenish look.
anatomy.zh: 西印度海牛是大型灰色桶状的哺乳动物，体长可达4米，体重超过500公斤。它们有桨状的鳍肢、扁平的圆尾和小小的眼睛。它们厚实多皱的皮肤上经常覆盖着藻类，让它们看起来带有绿色。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Manatees are gentle herbivores that spend six to eight hours a day eating seagrass and other water plants. They are usually slow-moving and peaceful, and they must come to the surface to breathe every few minutes. Mother manatees are very caring and stay close to their calves for up to two years.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 海牛是温和的草食动物，每天花六到八个小时吃海草和其他水生植物。它们通常行动缓慢而温和，必须每隔几分钟浮出水面呼吸。海牛妈妈非常爱护幼崽，会在幼崽身边陪伴长达两年。

habitat_and_distribution.en: West Indian manatees live in warm, shallow coastal waters, rivers, and springs in the southeastern United States, the Caribbean, and Central America. In winter, they gather near warm-water springs and power plant outflows because they cannot survive in cold water. They need water warmer than 20 degrees Celsius to stay healthy.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 西印度海牛生活在美国东南部、加勒比海和中美洲的温暖浅海水域、河流和泉水中。冬天它们会聚集在温泉和发电厂排水口附近，因为它们无法在寒冷的水中生存。它们需要水温高于20摄氏度才能保持健康。

habitat.text_en: Warm coastal waters and springs
habitat.text_zh: 温暖的沿海水域和泉水

description.en: Recognizable by their leathery armor shell, they are excellent diggers that feed mainly on insects.
description.zh: 以其坚韧的铠甲外壳而容易辨认，它们是出色的挖掘者，主要以昆虫为食。

anatomy.en: The nine-banded armadillo is about the size of a large cat, weighing 2.5 to 6.5 kilograms. Its body is covered in bony plates that form a hard, armor-like shell with nine movable bands around its middle. It has a long, pointed snout, big ears, and sharp claws made for digging.
anatomy.zh: 九带犰狳大约有一只大猫那么大，体重2.5到6.5公斤。它的身体覆盖着骨质甲片，形成坚硬的铠甲状外壳，中间有九条可活动的带。它有长长的尖鼻子、大耳朵和用于挖掘的锋利爪子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Nine-banded armadillos are mostly nocturnal and eat ants, beetles, and other insects by digging them out of the ground with their strong claws. An amazing fact is that a mother armadillo almost always gives birth to four identical quadruplets! When startled, they can jump straight up in the air.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 九带犰狳大多在夜间活动，用强壮的爪子从地下挖出蚂蚁、甲虫和其他昆虫来吃。一个惊人的事实是，犰狳妈妈几乎总是一胎生下四个一模一样的四胞胎！受到惊吓时，它们会直直地跳到空中。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Nine-banded armadillos prefer warm, rainy areas with soft soil for digging, such as forests, scrublands, and grasslands. They are found across the southern United States, Mexico, Central America, and much of South America. They have been steadily expanding their range northward in the United States.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 九带犰狳喜欢温暖多雨、土壤松软适合挖掘的地方，如森林、灌木丛和草原。它们分布在美国南部、墨西哥、中美洲和南美洲大部分地区。在美国，它们的分布范围一直在稳步向北扩展。

habitat.text_en: Warm, rainy environments
habitat.text_zh: 温暖多雨的环境

description.en: Famous for their black and white stripes and their ability to spray a strong-smelling liquid for defense.
description.zh: 以其黑白条纹和喷射强烈气味液体进行防御的能力而闻名。

anatomy.en: Striped skunks are about the size of a house cat, weighing 2 to 5.5 kilograms. They have glossy black fur with two bold white stripes running from their head down their back and onto their fluffy tail. Under their tail are special glands that can spray a terrible-smelling oily liquid up to 3 meters away.
anatomy.zh: 条纹臭鼬大约有一只家猫那么大，体重2到5.5公斤。它们有光亮的黑色毛皮，从头部沿着背部一直到蓬松的尾巴有两条醒目的白色条纹。在尾巴下面有特殊的腺体，能将一种气味极其难闻的油性液体喷射到3米远的地方。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Striped skunks are omnivores that eat insects, fruits, eggs, and small animals. Before spraying, a skunk will stomp its feet and raise its tail as a warning. They are mostly active at night and spend the winter sleeping in underground dens, sometimes sharing them with other skunks to stay warm.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 条纹臭鼬是杂食动物，吃昆虫、水果、鸡蛋和小动物。在喷射之前，臭鼬会跺脚并竖起尾巴作为警告。它们主要在夜间活动，冬天在地下洞穴中睡觉，有时会和其他臭鼬共用洞穴来保暖。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Striped skunks live in a wide variety of habitats including forests, grasslands, farmlands, and even cities across North America. They range from southern Canada throughout the United States to northern Mexico. They are very adaptable and can make their homes almost anywhere with food and shelter.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 条纹臭鼬生活在各种各样的栖息地，包括北美洲的森林、草原、农田甚至城市。它们的分布范围从加拿大南部到整个美国，一直延伸到墨西哥北部。它们适应能力很强，几乎在任何有食物和藏身之处的地方都能安家。

habitat.text_en: Open areas with a mixture of habitats
habitat.text_zh: 具有混合栖息地的开阔地区

description.en: Gray wolves are highly social apex predators that live and hunt in family groups called packs.
description.zh: 灰狼是高度社会化的顶级掠食者，以被称为「狼群」的家族群体生活和狩猎。

anatomy.en: Gray wolves are the largest wild members of the dog family, weighing 30 to 80 kilograms. They have powerful jaws, long legs built for running, and thick fur that can be gray, black, white, or brown. Their large paws act like snowshoes, helping them walk on top of deep snow.
anatomy.zh: 灰狼是犬科中体型最大的野生成员，体重30到80公斤。它们有强有力的下颌、适合奔跑的长腿和厚厚的毛皮，毛色可以是灰色、黑色、白色或棕色。它们的大脚掌就像雪鞋一样，帮助它们在深雪上行走。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Wolves live in packs of 5 to 10 members led by a breeding pair. They hunt together to take down large prey like elk and deer, using teamwork and clever strategies. Wolves communicate with each other through howling, body language, and scent marking, and their howls can be heard up to 16 kilometers away.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 狼群通常由5到10个成员组成，由一对繁殖配偶领导。它们协同狩猎，用团队合作和聪明的策略捕捉麋鹿和鹿等大型猎物。狼通过嚎叫、身体语言和气味标记来相互交流，它们的嚎叫声最远可以传到16公里之外。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Gray wolves once roamed across most of North America, Europe, and Asia. Today they live in tundra, forests, mountains, and grasslands in remote areas. In the United States, they are found mainly in Alaska, the northern Rocky Mountains, and the Great Lakes region.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 灰狼曾经在北美洲、欧洲和亚洲的大部分地区漫游。如今它们生活在偏远地区的苔原、森林、山脉和草原上。在美国，它们主要分布在阿拉斯加、北落基山脉和五大湖地区。

habitat.text_en: Tundra, woodlands, and forests
habitat.text_zh: 苔原、林地和森林

description.en: These social rodents live in large colonies called 'towns' beneath the surface of the prairies.
description.zh: 这些群居啮齿动物生活在草原地表下被称为「城镇」的大型聚居地中。

anatomy.en: Black-tailed prairie dogs are chubby, tan-colored rodents about 35 to 43 centimeters long, including their short black-tipped tail. They weigh around 0.7 to 1.4 kilograms. They have small ears, dark eyes, and short but strong legs with sharp claws for digging complex underground tunnels.
anatomy.zh: 黑尾土拨鼠是圆胖的棕褐色啮齿动物，全长约35到43厘米，包括它们那条末端为黑色的短尾巴。体重大约0.7到1.4公斤。它们有小耳朵、黑色眼睛和短而有力的腿，爪子锐利，用来挖掘复杂的地下隧道。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Prairie dogs eat grasses, seeds, and small insects. They are extremely social and live in family groups called coteries within their large underground towns. They have a special alarm bark that can describe the size, shape, color, and speed of an approaching predator, making their language one of the most complex in the animal kingdom.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 土拨鼠吃草、种子和小昆虫。它们非常善于社交，在大型地下城镇中以被称为「家族」的小组生活。它们有一种特殊的警报叫声，能够描述靠近的捕食者的大小、形状、颜色和速度，这使得它们的语言成为动物界中最复杂的之一。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Black-tailed prairie dogs live on the short-grass prairies of the Great Plains of North America. Their range stretches from southern Canada through the central United States to northern Mexico. Their underground towns can cover hundreds of hectares and contain thousands of individuals.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 黑尾土拨鼠生活在北美大平原的短草草原上。它们的分布范围从加拿大南部穿过美国中部一直延伸到墨西哥北部。它们的地下城镇可以覆盖数百公顷的面积，容纳数千只个体。

habitat.text_en: Shortgrass prairies
habitat.text_zh: 短草草原

description.en: A powerful and ferocious animal that resembles a small bear but is actually the largest land-dwelling weasel.
description.zh: 一种强大而凶猛的动物，外表像小熊，但实际上是体型最大的陆生鼬科动物。

anatomy.en: Wolverines are stocky, muscular animals weighing 9 to 25 kilograms, about the size of a medium dog. They have thick, dark brown fur with lighter stripes along their sides and a broad, rounded head with strong jaws. Their large paws spread wide like snowshoes, helping them move easily through deep snow.
anatomy.zh: 狼獾是粗壮而肌肉发达的动物，体重9到25公斤，大约有一只中型犬那么大。它们有厚厚的深棕色毛皮，体侧有较浅的条纹，头部宽而圆，下颌强壮有力。它们的大脚掌像雪鞋一样张开，帮助它们在深雪中轻松移动。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Wolverines are fierce and fearless, sometimes driving bears and mountain lions away from their food. They eat a variety of foods including carrion, small mammals, birds, and berries. They are solitary animals that roam enormous territories, sometimes traveling 24 kilometers in a single day to find food.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 狼獾凶猛无畏，有时会把熊和美洲狮从它们的食物旁边赶走。它们吃各种食物，包括腐肉、小型哺乳动物、鸟类和浆果。它们是独居动物，活动领地非常广阔，有时一天内会走24公里来寻找食物。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Wolverines live in cold, remote boreal forests, alpine tundra, and mountain regions of the Northern Hemisphere. In North America, they are found mainly in Alaska, northern Canada, and parts of the northern Rocky Mountains. They need large areas of wilderness with persistent snow cover and are very sensitive to human disturbance.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 狼獾生活在北半球寒冷偏远的北方针叶林、高山苔原和山区。在北美洲，它们主要分布在阿拉斯加、加拿大北部和北落基山脉的部分地区。它们需要大面积有持续积雪覆盖的荒野，对人类的干扰非常敏感。

habitat.text_en: Boreal forests and alpine tundra
habitat.text_zh: 北方针叶林和高山苔原

description.en: A stout, low-slung mammal known for its digging abilities and distinctive facial stripes.
description.zh: 一种粗壮低矮的哺乳动物，以其挖掘能力和独特的面部条纹而闻名。

anatomy.en: American badgers have flat, wedge-shaped bodies built close to the ground, weighing 6 to 12 kilograms. They have short, powerful legs with long front claws that can be up to 5 centimeters long, perfect for digging. Their face has a striking black and white pattern with a white stripe running from their nose over the top of their head.
anatomy.zh: 美洲獾有扁平的楔形身体，紧贴地面，体重6到12公斤。它们有短而有力的腿，前爪可长达5厘米，非常适合挖掘。它们的面部有醒目的黑白花纹，一条白色条纹从鼻子延伸到头顶。

ecology_and_behavior.en: American badgers are expert diggers that hunt ground squirrels, prairie dogs, and other burrowing animals by digging them out of their tunnels at amazing speed. They sometimes team up with coyotes to hunt together, where the coyote chases prey above ground while the badger digs below. They are mostly active at night and are known for being tough and fearless when cornered.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 美洲獾是挖掘高手，它们以惊人的速度将地松鼠、土拨鼠和其他穴居动物从洞穴中挖出来捕食。它们有时会和郊狼合作狩猎，郊狼在地面上追赶猎物，而獾在地下挖掘。它们主要在夜间活动，被逼到角落时以强悍无畏著称。

habitat_and_distribution.en: American badgers live in dry, open grasslands, prairies, and farmlands across western and central North America. They range from south-central Canada through the western United States to central Mexico. They prefer areas with loose, sandy soil that is easy to dig through.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲獾生活在北美洲西部和中部干燥开阔的草原、大草原和农田中。它们的分布范围从加拿大中南部到美国西部，一直延伸到墨西哥中部。它们喜欢松软的沙质土壤，便于挖掘。

habitat.text_en: Dry, open grasslands
habitat.text_zh: 干燥开阔的草原

description.en: Equipped with thick white coats and special hooves, they easily navigate extreme alpine environments.
description.zh: 配备厚厚的白色皮毛和特殊的蹄子，它们能轻松在极端高山环境中穿梭。

anatomy.en: Mountain goats stand about 1 meter tall at the shoulder and weigh 45 to 140 kilograms, with males being larger than females. They are covered in thick, woolly white fur that keeps them warm in freezing temperatures. Their hooves have hard outer edges and soft, rubbery pads underneath that grip rocky surfaces like climbing shoes.
anatomy.zh: 雪羊肩高约1米，体重45到140公斤，雄性比雌性更大。它们全身覆盖着厚厚的白色羊毛，能在冰冻的温度中保暖。它们的蹄子外缘坚硬，底部有柔软的橡胶状肉垫，能像攀岩鞋一样抓住岩石表面。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Mountain goats eat grasses, mosses, lichens, and other alpine plants. They are incredible climbers that can scale nearly vertical cliff faces to escape predators like eagles and mountain lions. In spring, they travel to natural salt licks to get important minerals their bodies need.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 雪羊吃草、苔藓、地衣和其他高山植物。它们是令人难以置信的攀爬高手，能攀上几乎垂直的悬崖来躲避鹰和美洲狮等捕食者。春天，它们会去天然盐碱地舔食身体所需的重要矿物质。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Mountain goats live high up in the steep, rocky mountains of western North America, usually above the tree line. They are found from southeastern Alaska through British Columbia and into the northern Rocky Mountains of the United States. They prefer the most rugged, inaccessible peaks where few other animals can survive.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 雪羊生活在北美洲西部陡峭的岩石山峰高处，通常在树线以上。它们分布从阿拉斯加东南部到不列颠哥伦比亚省，再到美国北部落基山脉。它们偏爱最崎岖、最难以到达的山峰，那里很少有其他动物能够生存。

habitat.text_en: High alpine regions
habitat.text_zh: 高山地区

description.en: The red panda is a small tree-dwelling mammal known for its reddish-brown fur and long, bushy tail.
description.zh: 小熊猫是一种小型的树栖哺乳动物，以其红褐色的皮毛和长而蓬松的尾巴而闻名。

anatomy.en: Red pandas are slightly larger than a domestic cat, weighing about 3 to 6 kilograms. They have thick reddish-brown fur, a black belly, and a ringed tail that they use as a warm blanket in the cold. They also have a 'false thumb', which is actually an extended wrist bone used to grip bamboo tightly.
anatomy.zh: 小熊猫比家猫稍大一点，体重约3到6公斤。它们有厚厚的红褐色皮毛、黑色的腹部和带环纹的尾巴，在寒冷时可以当温暖的毯子用。它们还有一个“伪拇指”，实际上是一根伸长的腕骨，用来紧紧抓取竹子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Despite being classified as carnivores, red pandas mostly eat bamboo leaves and shoots, along with some fruit and insects. They are excellent climbers and spend most of their lives high up in trees. They are mostly solitary animals and are most active at dawn and dusk.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 尽管被分类为食肉动物，小熊猫主要吃竹叶和竹笋，也会吃一些水果和昆虫。它们是出色的攀爬者，一生中大部分时间都在高高的树上度过。它们大多是独居动物，在黎明和黄昏时最为活跃。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Red pandas live in high-altitude temperate forests with bamboo understories in the Himalayas and high mountains of southwestern China. They thrive in cold climates and steep mountainous terrain. Their populations are unfortunately declining due to habitat loss.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 小熊猫生活在喜马拉雅山脉和中国西南部高山地区，那些带有竹林底层的的高海拔温带森林中。它们在寒冷的气候和陡峭的山地地形中繁衍生息。遗憾的是，由于栖息地丧失，它们的数量正在下降。

habitat.text_en: Himalayan and Southwestern Chinese forests
habitat.text_zh: 喜马拉雅及中国西南森林

description.en: The African bush elephant is the largest living land animal in the world, known for its enormous ears and long trunk.
description.zh: 非洲草原象是世界上最大的现存陆地动物，以其巨大的耳朵和长长的鼻子而闻名。

anatomy.en: African bush elephants are massive, weighing up to 6,000 kilograms! They have huge ears shaped like the continent of Africa, which help them stay cool in the hot sun. Their long trunk is actually a nose and upper lip joined together, used for breathing, drinking, and grabbing food.
anatomy.zh: 非洲草原象体型巨大，体重可达6000公斤！它们巨大的耳朵形状像非洲大陆，能帮它们在烈日下散热。它们长长的鼻子其实是鼻子和上唇连在一起的，用来呼吸、喝水和抓取食物。

ecology_and_behavior.en: These gentle giants eat grasses, leaves, and bark, spending up to 18 hours a day feeding. They live in close-knit family groups led by an older, wise female called a matriarch. Elephants are very smart and can communicate with each other using low rumbles that travel far underground.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 这些温和的庞然大物吃草、树叶和树皮，每天花长达18个小时进食。它们生活在紧密的家庭群体中，由一只年长聪明的雌象（称为女族长）带领。大象非常聪明，能用在地下传播得很远的低沉隆隆声相互交流。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They roam across the grassy savannas, woodlands, and deserts of Sub-Saharan Africa. They need to live near water sources because they drink up to 190 liters of water a day. Sadly, they are threatened by habitat loss and poaching for their ivory tusks.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们漫步在撒哈拉以南非洲的稀树草原、林地和沙漠中。它们必须住在水源附近，因为每天要喝多达190升的水。遗憾的是，它们正受到栖息地丧失和为获取象牙而进行盗猎的威胁。

habitat.text_en: Sub-Saharan African Savannas
habitat.text_zh: 撒哈拉以南非洲稀树草原

description.en: Lions are powerful big cats known as the 'kings of the savanna', and are the only cats that live in large family groups.
description.zh: 狮子是强壮的大型猫科动物，被称为“草原之王”，也是唯一群居的大型猫科动物。

anatomy.en: Lions are huge cats with strong bodies and golden-yellow fur. Male lions grow a thick, impressive mane of hair around their necks, which makes them look bigger and protects them in fights. Their roar is so loud it can be heard from 8 kilometers away!
anatomy.zh: 狮子是拥有强壮身体和金黄色皮毛的大型猫科动物。雄狮的脖子周围会长出厚厚而引人注目的鬣毛，这让它们看起来更大，也能在打斗时保护它们。它们的吼声非常响亮，在8公里外都能听到！

ecology_and_behavior.en: Unlike other cats, lions are very social and live in groups called prides, which are mostly made up of females and their cubs. The lionesses do most of the hunting, working as a team to catch fast prey like zebras and wildebeests. Lions love to rest and can sleep for up to 20 hours a day!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 与其他猫科动物不同，狮子非常喜欢群居，生活在被称为“狮群”的群体中，主要由雌狮和它们的幼崽组成。雌狮负责大部分的狩猎，它们团队合作捕捉斑马和角马等速度很快的猎物。狮子很爱休息，每天能睡20个小时！

habitat_and_distribution.en: Most wild lions live in the open savannas and grassy plains of Africa, where their golden fur camouflages perfectly with the dry grass. A very small population also lives in the Gir Forest of India. They need large areas of land with plenty of prey to survive.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 大多数野生狮子生活在非洲开阔的稀树草原上，那里金黄色的毛皮能完美地隐蔽在干草中。在印度的吉尔森林中也生活着极小一部分种群。它们需要大片有丰富猎物的土地才能生存。

habitat.text_en: African Savannas and Indian Gir Forest
habitat.text_zh: 非洲稀树草原与印度吉尔森林

description.en: The cheetah is the fastest land animal on Earth, capable of reaching highway speeds in just a few seconds.
description.zh: 猎豹是地球上跑得最快的陆地动物，能在几秒钟内达到高速公路上的行驶速度。

anatomy.en: Cheetahs are built for extreme speed. They have a slim body, a flexible spine, and long, thin legs. Their fur is covered in solid black spots, and they have distinct black 'tear marks' running from their eyes down to their mouth, which help block the glare of the sun.
anatomy.zh: 猎豹天生就是为了极速而生的。它们有苗条的身体、灵活的脊椎和细长的腿。它们的皮毛上布满了纯黑色的斑点，从眼睛到嘴巴有明显的黑色“泪痕”，这有助于遮挡太阳的刺眼光线。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Cheetahs hunt during the day to avoid competing with lions and leopards. Instead of sneaking up close, they rely on short, explosive sprints to chase down prey like gazelles, reaching speeds of up to 110 kilometers per hour! After a fast run, they need to rest to catch their breath.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 猎豹在白天狩猎，以避免与狮子和豹子竞争。它们不靠偷偷靠近，而是依靠短暂爆发性的冲刺来追捕瞪羚等猎物，速度可达每小时110公里！快速奔跑后，它们需要休息来喘口气。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They mainly live in the wide, open savannas and dry areas of Sub-Saharan Africa, with a very small number found in Iran. They need wide open spaces so they can use their super speed without crashing into trees or bushes.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们主要生活在撒哈拉以南非洲广阔的稀树草原和干燥地区，在伊朗也能找到极少数量的个体。它们需要广阔的开阔地带，这样才能施展它们的超级速度而不会撞到树木或灌木丛上。

habitat.text_en: Open savannas of Africa
habitat.text_zh: 非洲开阔稀树草原

description.en: Plains zebras are horse-like animals famous for their black-and-white striped coats.
description.zh: 平原斑马是长得像马的动物，以它们黑白相间的条纹外套而闻名。

anatomy.en: Zebras look a lot like horses but are slightly smaller and stockier. Their most famous feature is their black and white stripes. Just like human fingerprints, no two zebras have the exact same stripe pattern! The stripes help confuse predators and keep away biting flies.
anatomy.zh: 斑马长得很像马，但稍微小一点、粗壮一点。它们最著名的特征就是黑白相间的条纹。就像人类的指纹一样，没有两只斑马的条纹图案是完全相同的！这些条纹有助于迷惑捕食者，还能驱赶咬人的苍蝇。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Zebras are grazers that spend most of their day eating tough savanna grasses. They are highly social and live in small family groups, which often join together to form massive herds. When resting, they usually stand next to each other facing opposite directions so they can watch out for danger from all sides.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 斑马是食草动物，一天中大部分时间都在吃坚硬的草原草。它们非常合群，生活在小家庭中，这些家庭经常聚在一起形成巨大的兽群。休息时，它们通常并排站着，面朝相反的方向，这样就能观察四面八方的危险。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Plains zebras are the most common wild horses in the world. They live across the grasslands, savannas, and lightly wooded areas of eastern and southern Africa. Every year, huge numbers of zebras travel long distances across the Serengeti in search of fresh grass and water.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 平原斑马是世界上最常见的野马。它们分布在非洲东部和南部的草原、稀树草原和树木稀疏的地区。每年都有大量的斑马在塞伦盖蒂进行长途迁徙，寻找新鲜的草和水。

habitat.text_en: Grasslands of Eastern and Southern Africa
habitat.text_zh: 非洲东南部草原

description.en: The hippopotamus is a huge, water-loving mammal with a massive mouth, spending its days keeping cool in African rivers.
description.zh: 河马是一种喜爱水的巨型哺乳动物，嘴巴极大，白天通常在非洲的河流里乘凉。

anatomy.en: Hippos are the third-largest land mammals, weighing up to 3,000 kilograms! They have a barrel-shaped body, short legs, and an enormous head. Their eyes, ears, and nose are placed high on top of their head so they can see, hear, and breathe while the rest of their body is hidden underwater.
anatomy.zh: 河马是第三大陸地哺乳动物，体重可达3000公斤！它们身体呈桶状，腿很短，头巨大。它们的眼睛、耳朵和鼻子长在头顶很高的地方，这样它们就可以在身体其他部分藏在水下时，依然能够看、听和呼吸。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Even though they look cute, hippos are very aggressive and fiercely defend their river territory. During the hot day, they stay in the water to keep their sensitive skin from sunburn. At night, they leave the water to walk onto the land and eat large amounts of grass.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 虽然它们看起来很可爱，但河马非常有攻击性，会激烈地保卫自己的河流领地。在炎热的白天，它们呆在水里，以免敏感的皮肤被晒伤。到了晚上，它们离开水走到陆地上吃大量的草。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Hippopotamuses live in rivers, lakes, and mangrove swamps across Sub-Saharan Africa. They need areas with slow-moving water to rest in during the day and grassy shores nearby for feeding at night. Their numbers have gone down because they are losing their watery homes.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 河马生活在撒哈拉以南非洲的河流、湖泊和红树林沼泽中。它们需要白天能在水流缓慢的地方休息，晚上在附近的草岸上觅食。由于失去了水中的家园，它们的数量已经减少。

habitat.text_en: Rivers and lakes of Sub-Saharan Africa
habitat.text_zh: 撒哈拉以南非洲的河流与湖泊

description.en: The blue whale is a marine mammal and the largest animal known to have ever existed on Earth, relying on baleen plates to filter feed.
description.zh: 蓝鲸是一种海洋哺乳动物，也是已知地球上生存过的体型最大的动物，依靠鲸须板进行过滤进食。

anatomy.en: Blue whales are true ocean giants! They can grow up to 100 feet long and weigh as much as 33 elephants. A blue whale's heart is about the size of a small car, and its tongue weighs as much as an entire elephant. Instead of sharp teeth, they have giant fringed plates called baleen hanging from their upper jaws.
anatomy.zh: 蓝鲸是真正的海洋巨无霸！它们可以长达100英尺，体重相当于33头大象的总和。蓝鲸的心脏大约有一辆小汽车那么大，而它的舌头和大象一样重。它们没有锋利的牙齿，上颚悬挂着巨大的边缘带有流苏的鲸须板。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Despite being the biggest creatures on the planet, blue whales survive by eating some of the smallest animals: tiny shrimp-like creatures called krill. A single blue whale can eat up to 4 tons of krill a day! They are also incredibly loud, communicating through deep, low-frequency rumbles that can travel for thousands of miles underwater.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 尽管是地球上最大的生物，蓝鲸却靠吃最小的动物生存：一种叫做磷虾的微小虾类。一只蓝鲸一天能吃掉多达4吨的磷虾！它们的声音也大得惊人，通过深沉的低频隆隆声进行交流，这种声音在水下可以传播数千英里。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Blue whales roam almost all of the world's deep oceans. During the summer, they travel to the cold, icy waters near the North and South Poles where food is abundant. In the winter, they migrate thousands of miles to warmer tropical waters to give birth to their giant babies.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 蓝鲸游弋在世界各地几乎所有的深海大洋中。在夏天，它们前往南北极附近食物丰富的寒冷冰雪水域。到了冬天，它们会游行数千英里迁徙到温暖的热带水域，在那里生下巨大的鲸鱼宝宝。

habitat.text_en: Global Oceans, polar feeding grounds to tropical breeding grounds
habitat.text_zh: 全球各大洋，从极地觅食区到热带繁殖区

description.en: The orca, also known as the killer whale, is a highly intelligent toothed whale and the largest member of the oceanic dolphin family.
description.zh: 虎鲸也被称为杀人鲸，是一种高度聪明的齿鲸，也是海洋海豚科中体型最大的成员。

anatomy.en: Orcas have a very famous black back, a white chest, and a white patch above and behind their eyes. Male orcas have a huge dorsal fin on their back that can stand up to 6 feet tall! They have a sleek, torpedo-like body covered in a thick layer of fat called blubber to stay warm.
anatomy.zh: 虎鲸拥有非常著名的黑色背部、白色胸部，以及眼睛后上方的一块白色斑块。雄性虎鲸背部有一个巨大的背鳍，可以高达6英尺！它们拥有流线型的鱼雷状身体，表面覆盖着一层被称为鲸脂的厚脂肪以保持温暖。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Often called the 'wolves of the sea,' orcas are apex predators, meaning nothing else hunts them. They live in tight family groups called pods and are incredibly smart. They work together to hunt anything from fish and penguins to seals and even large whales. Each family pod speaks its own unique language of clicks and whistles.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 虎鲸常被称为「海中之狼」，它们是顶级捕食者，这意味着没有任何动物会捕杀它们。它们生活在被称为「社群」的紧密家庭群体中，非常聪明。它们合作捕猎，从鱼类、企鹅到海豹甚至大型鲸鱼。每个家庭社群都说着自己独有的点击声和口哨声语言。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Orcas are one of the most widely distributed mammals in the world. They can be found in every ocean on Earth, from the freezing icy waters of the Arctic and Antarctica to warm tropical seas, though they prefer colder coastal areas.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 虎鲸是世界上分布最广的哺乳动物之一。地球上的每一个大洋里都能找到它们的身影，从北极和南极极其寒冷的冰水域到温暖的热带海洋，尽管它们更喜欢寒冷的沿海地区。

habitat.text_en: Global Oceans, heavily concentrated in coastal high latitudes
habitat.text_zh: 全球海洋，高度集中在高纬度沿海地区

description.en: The common bottlenose dolphin is a highly social and intelligent marine mammal, recognized globally for its characteristic 'smiling' beak.
description.zh: 宽吻海豚是一种高度群居且聪明的海洋哺乳动物，以其标志性的「微笑」长喙而在全球闻名。

anatomy.en: These dolphins are mostly gray with a lighter belly. They have a short, thick snout that looks a bit like a bottle, giving them their name! Because of the way their mouth curves, they always look like they are smiling. They breathe air through a single blowhole on the top of their heads.
anatomy.zh: 这些海豚大多是灰色的，腹部颜色较浅。它们有一个又短又粗的吻部，看起来有点像瓶子，它们的名字就是这么来的！因为它们嘴巴弯曲的形状，它们看起来总是在微笑。它们通过头顶的一个呼吸孔来呼吸空气。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bottlenose dolphins are playful and super smart. They use a superpower called echolocation—making clicking sounds that bounce off objects—to find fish hidden in muddy water. When they sleep, they shut down only half of their brain and keep one eye open to watch for sharks and remember to go up for air!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 宽吻海豚爱玩且超级聪明。它们使用一种叫做回声定位的超能力——发出咔哒声，声音从物体上弹回——来寻找隐藏在浑水中的鱼。当它们睡觉时，只关闭一半的大脑，并睁着一只眼睛提防鲨鱼，同时记住要浮出水面呼吸！

habitat_and_distribution.en: You won't find them in the freezing ice, but bottlenose dolphins live in almost all the warm and temperate oceans, gulfs, and seas around the world. They love surfing in the waves near the beach.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 你不会在冰冷的冰层中找到它们，但宽吻海豚几乎生活在全世界所有的温水和温带海洋、海湾及海域中。它们喜欢在海滩附近的波浪中冲浪。

habitat.text_en: Temperate and tropical coastal waters and open oceans
habitat.text_zh: 温带和热带近海水域及开阔海洋

description.en: The polar bear is a massive hypercarnivorous bear whose native range lies largely within the Arctic Circle, heavily dependent on sea ice.
description.zh: 北极熊是一种巨大的超肉食性熊类，其原生栖息地主要在北极圈内，高度依赖海冰生存。

anatomy.en: Did you know a polar bear's fur isn't actually white? It's transparent and hollow, reflecting the sunlight! Under all that fluffy hair, their skin is jet black to absorb the sun's heat. They have massive paws the size of dinner plates that act like snowshoes on ice and paddles in the water.
anatomy.zh: 你知道北极熊的毛其实不是白色的吗？它是透明且中空的，只是反射了阳光！在所有毛茸茸的毛发下面，它们的皮肤是乌黑的，用来吸收太阳的热量。它们长着餐盘大小的巨爪，在冰上就像雪地靴，在水里就像船桨。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Polar bears are the largest meat-eating animals that live on land. They are patient hunters, waiting by breathing holes in the ice to catch ringed seals. A polar bear's nose is so amazing that it can smell a seal hiding under three feet of solid snow from almost a mile away!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 北极熊是生活在陆地上的体型最大的食肉动物。它们是耐心的猎手，守在冰层的呼吸孔旁捕捉环斑海豹。北极熊的鼻子非常神奇，能从近一英里外闻到躲在三英尺厚实雪下的海豹！

habitat_and_distribution.en: These majestic bears live exclusively in the freezing Arctic, roaming across the sea ice of Canada, Russia, Alaska, Greenland, and Norway. Sadly, because the planet is getting warmer, the ice is melting fast, which makes it harder for them to find food and survive.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 这些雄伟的熊专属于冰冷的北极，漫游在加拿大、俄罗斯、阿拉斯加、格陵兰岛和挪威的海冰上。可悲的是，因为地球正在变暖，冰层融化得很快，这使得它们寻找食物和生存变得更加困难。

habitat.text_en: Circumpolar Arctic sea ice and coastal tundra
habitat.text_zh: 环北极海冰带与沿海冻原

description.en: The walrus is a large flippered marine mammal recognized by its prominent tusks and whiskers, inhabiting shallow Arctic seas.
description.zh: 海象是一种体型巨大、长有鳍状肢的海洋哺乳动物，以其突出的长牙和胡须为特征，栖息在浅水北极海域。

anatomy.en: You can always recognize a walrus by its two giant, white tusks and a thick, bristly mustache! Both male and female walruses grow these tusks, which can reach up to 3 feet long. Their skin is thick, wrinkly, and pinkish-brown, covering a heavy layer of fat to survive the freezing cold.
anatomy.zh: 你总是能通过两根巨大的白色长牙和浓密的胡须认出海象！雄性和雌性海象都会长出这种可以长达3英尺的长牙。它们的皮肤又厚又皱，呈粉棕色，下面覆盖着厚厚的一层脂肪以抵御严寒。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Walruses use their giant tusks like ice picks to pull their heavy bodies out of the water onto the ice. They love to rest together in huge, noisy herds, bumping and barking at each other. For food, they dive to the dark, muddy ocean floor and use their sensitive whiskers to find thousands of clams and snails.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 海象用它们巨大的长牙像冰镐一样，把沉重的身体从水里拉到冰上。它们喜欢成群结队地在冰上休息，非常喧闹，互相碰撞和吠叫。为了觅食，它们潜入黑暗、泥泞的海底，用敏感的胡须寻找成千上万的蛤蜊和蜗牛。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Walruses live in the shallow waters just above the ocean floor around the North Pole. They need strong sea ice to use as floating resting beds and a safe place to have their babies.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 海象生活在北极点周围靠近海底的浅水区。它们需要坚固的海冰作为漂浮的休息床，也是生宝宝的安全地带。

habitat.text_en: Shallow Arctic continental shelves and pack ice
habitat.text_zh: 北极大陆架浅水区及浮冰区

description.en: The emperor penguin is the tallest and heaviest of all penguin species, renowned for breeding during the brutal Antarctic winter.
description.zh: 帝企鹅是所有企鹅物种中最高和最重的，以在残酷的南极冬季进行繁殖而闻名。

anatomy.en: Standing nearly 4 feet tall and weighing up to 90 pounds, Emperor penguins look like giant birds dressed in elegant tuxedos with a beautiful glow of golden-yellow feathers around their necks. They have a special warm pouch on their belly to hold their eggs.
anatomy.zh: 帝企鹅站立时近4英尺高，重达90磅，看起来像穿着优雅燕尾服的巨型鸟类，脖子周围闪耀着美丽的淡黄色羽毛光泽。它们的腹部有一个温暖的特殊皮褶，用来存放它们的蛋。

ecology_and_behavior.en: These penguins are the toughest parents on Earth! While the moms travel far to the ocean to hunt for fish, the dads stay behind on the freezing ice. A dad balances the egg on his feet to keep it from touching the ice, standing in the bitter dark winter for two whole months without eating! To stay warm, thousands of penguins huddle tightly together in a giant circle.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 这些企鹅是地球上最坚强的父母！当妈妈们前往遥远的海洋捕鱼时，爸爸们留在了冰天雪地里。爸爸把蛋平衡在脚上以防接触到冰面，在寒冷黑暗的冬天里整整站立两个月不吃东西！为了保暖，成千上万只企鹅紧紧依偎在一起，围成一个巨大的圆圈。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Emperor penguins live on the thick, frozen sea ice connected to the continent of Antarctica. They spend their time diving deep into the frigid Southern Ocean to catch fish and squid.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 帝企鹅生活在连接着南极洲大陆的厚重且冰冻的海冰上。它们花时间深潜入寒冷的南大洋去捕捉鱼和乌贼。

habitat.text_en: Antarctic fast ice and Southern Ocean waters
habitat.text_zh: 南极固定冰及南大洋水域

description.en: The peregrine falcon is a cosmopolitan bird of prey renowned for its incredible speed, making it the fastest animal on the planet.
description.zh: 游隼是一种世界性分布的猛禽，以其惊人的速度而闻名，是地球上最快的动物。

anatomy.en: Peregrine falcons have slate blue-gray wings, a white chest with dark stripes, and a black face pattern that looks like a knight's helmet. Their bodies are built for extreme speed, shaped like a fighter jet, with special bones in their nose that let them breathe even when flying through the air like a rocket.
anatomy.zh: 游隼有石板蓝灰色的翅膀，白色的胸部带有深色条纹，黑色的面部图案看起来像骑士的头盔。它们的身体专为极速而生，形状像战斗机，鼻子里有特殊的骨头，让它们在像火箭一样在空中飞行时仍能呼吸。

ecology_and_behavior.en: They hold the record as the fastest animals on Earth! When a peregrine falcon spots a yummy pigeon far below, it folds its wings and dives straight down in a move called a 'stoop.' During this dive, they can reach speeds of over 240 miles per hour (386 km/h)—faster than a race car!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们保持着地球上最快动物的纪录！当一只游隼在下方很远处发现美味的鸽子时，它会收起翅膀径直向下俯冲，这个动作叫做「stoop」。在这个俯冲过程中，它们的速度可以达到每小时240英里（386公里/小时）以上——比赛车还快！

habitat_and_distribution.en: These adaptable birds live on every continent except Antarctica. While they naturally nest on high mountain cliffs, many have moved into human cities, building their nests on top of towering skyscrapers and bridges.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 这些适应性极强的鸟类生活在除南极洲以外的各个大陆上。虽然它们天生喜欢在高山的悬崖峭壁上筑巢，但许多游隼已经搬进了人类的城市，在高耸的摩天大楼和桥梁顶部筑巢。

habitat.text_en: Global distribution across cliffs, tundras, and urban skyscrapers
habitat.text_zh: 广泛分布于全球悬崖、冻原及城市摩天大楼

description.en: The wandering albatross is a large seabird from the Southern Ocean, possessing the largest wingspan of any living bird.
description.zh: 漂泊信天翁是来自南大洋的大型海鸟，拥有现存鸟类中最大的翼展。

anatomy.en: If you stretched your arms out wide, you still wouldn't be as wide as a Wandering Albatross! They have the largest wingspan of any bird alive, measuring up to 11.5 feet (3.5 meters) from tip to tip. They have a big hooked pink beak perfect for grabbing slippery food from the water.
anatomy.zh: 如果你把双臂完全伸展开，还是没有漂泊信天翁那么宽！它们拥有现存鸟类中最大的翼展，两端相距可达11.5英尺（3.5米）。它们有一张带钩的粉红色大嘴，非常适合从水中抓取滑溜溜的食物。

ecology_and_behavior.en: These magnificent birds are the ultimate ocean gliders. Thanks to their massive wings, they can lock their elbow joints and ride the ocean winds for hours without flapping their wings even once. They can fly around the entire Earth in a single year, only landing on the water to eat squid and fish.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 这些华丽的鸟类是终极的海洋滑翔机。多亏了巨大的翅膀，它们可以锁定肘关节，乘着海风飞行数小时而无需拍打一次翅膀。它们在一年内就能绕地球飞行一圈，只有在吃乌贼和鱼的时候才会降落在水面上。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They live almost entirely in the wild, windy skies of the Southern Ocean. They only ever return to solid ground on remote, rocky islands to build their nests and raise their fluffy chicks.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们几乎完全生活在南大洋狂风大作的野外天空中。它们只在偏远、岩石密布的岛屿上才会回到坚实的陆地上，为了筑巢和抚养毛茸茸的雏鸟。

habitat.text_en: Pelagic zones of the Southern Ocean and sub-Antarctic islands
habitat.text_zh: 南大洋远洋带及亚南极岛屿

description.en: The common ostrich is a species of flightless bird native to Africa, holding the record as the largest living bird.
description.zh: 非洲鸵鸟是一种原产于非洲的不会飞的鸟类，保持着体型最大现存鸟类的记录。

anatomy.en: Ostriches are the giants of the bird world, growing taller than the tallest human! They have long, bare necks, big fluffy black and white feathers on males, and massive eyes that are bigger than their own brains. To help them run, they only have two toes on each foot—one with a deadly sharp claw.
anatomy.zh: 鸵鸟是鸟类世界的巨人，长得比最高的人类还要高！它们有长长光秃秃的脖子，雄性身上长着巨大的黑白相间的蓬松羽毛，还有比它们自己大脑还要大的巨大眼睛。为了帮助奔跑，它们每只脚上只有两个脚趾——其中一个长着致命的利爪。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Ostriches might not be able to fly, but they sure can run! They are the fastest birds on land, sprinting at speeds up to 43 miles per hour (70 km/h). When faced with danger, like a hungry lion, an ostrich can deliver a super-powerful forward kick that is strong enough to save its life. They mostly munch on plants, seeds, and sometimes crunchy insects.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 鸵鸟也许不会飞，但它们绝对很能跑！它们是陆地上最快的鸟类，冲刺速度可达每小时43英里（70公里/小时）。当面临危险时，比如遇到饥饿的狮子，鸵鸟可以踢出超级有力的一脚，其力量足以保命。它们主要大口咀嚼植物、种子，有时也吃嘎嘣脆的昆虫。

habitat_and_distribution.en: You can spot ostriches running across the wide, dusty savannas and dry deserts of Africa. They like living in open spaces so their big eyes can spot sneaky predators from far away.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 你可以看到鸵鸟在非洲广阔、尘土飞扬的大草原和干旱的沙漠中奔跑。它们喜欢生活在开阔的地方，这样它们的大眼睛就能从很远的地方发现鬼鬼祟祟的捕食者。

habitat.text_en: African savannas, shrublands, and desert edges
habitat.text_zh: 非洲稀树草原、灌木丛及沙漠边缘

description.en: The greater flamingo is a tall, pink-plumaged wading bird widely distributed across the Old World.
description.zh: 大红鹳是一种身材高挑、长有粉红色羽毛的涉禽，广泛分布于旧大陆。

anatomy.en: With their incredibly long, skinny legs and an elegant, S-shaped neck, flamingos are hard to miss. Their feathers are a beautiful pinkish-white. Their beak has a very weird bend in the middle, designed to be used upside down in the water!
anatomy.zh: 拥有令人难以置信的细长双腿和优雅的S形脖子，火烈鸟让人很难错过。它们的羽毛是美丽的粉白色。它们的鸟喙中间有一个非常奇怪的弯曲，这是为了在水里倒过来用而设计的！

ecology_and_behavior.en: Why are flamingos pink? It's not magic, it's their diet! They eat algae and tiny brine shrimp that contain red color pigments. The more they eat, the pinker they get. A large gathering of these birds is fabulously called a 'flamboyance'. They also love to take a nap while standing perfectly balanced on just one leg.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 火烈鸟为什么是粉红色的？这不是魔法，而是它们的饮食！它们吃含有红色色素的藻类和微小的丰年虾。吃得越多，它们就变得越粉。一大群这种鸟被绝妙地称为「flamboyance（华丽群体）」。它们还喜欢在一只脚上保持完美平衡站着打个盹。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Greater flamingos live in big flocks in shallow, muddy, and very salty lakes or lagoons. You can find them across Africa, southern Europe, and parts of Asia. They like water that is so salty it would make other animals sick.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 大红鹳成大群生活在泥泞且盐度极高的浅水湖泊或泻湖中。你可以在非洲、南欧和亚洲的部分地区找到它们。它们喜欢咸得会让其他动物生病的水域。

habitat.text_en: Saline lakes, estuaries, and shallow coastal lagoons across Africa and Eurasia
habitat.text_zh: 横跨非洲和欧亚大陆的咸水湖、河口和浅水沿海泻湖

description.en: The green sea turtle is an endangered marine reptile that undertakes long migrations between feeding grounds and hatching beaches.
description.zh: 绿海龟是一种濒危的海洋爬行动物，在觅食地和孵化海滩之间进行长途迁徙。

anatomy.en: Green sea turtles have a smooth, teardrop-shaped shell that makes them excellent swimmers. Unlike cartoon turtles, sea turtles cannot pull their heads and flippers inside their shells. Their name is actually a secret—they are named 'green' because of the green fat hidden beneath their shell, not the color of the shell itself!
anatomy.zh: 绿海龟有一个光滑的水滴状外壳，使它们成为出色的游泳者。不像卡通里的乌龟，海龟不能把头和鳍缩进壳里。它们的名字其实是一个秘密——它们被称为「绿」海龟是因为藏在壳下的绿色脂肪，而不是壳本身的颜色！

ecology_and_behavior.en: While baby sea turtles might eat bugs and jellyfish, adult green sea turtles are vegetarians! They spend their days peacefully grazing on underwater meadows of sea grass. When a mother turtle is ready to lay eggs, she uses Earth's magnetic field to navigate thousands of miles back to the exact beach where she hatched as a baby.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 虽然小海龟可能会吃虫子和水母，但成年的绿海龟是素食主义者！它们平静地在水下海草甸上吃草度日。当海龟妈妈准备产卵时，她会利用地球磁场导航数千英里，准确地回到她小时候破壳而出的那片海滩。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They glide through the warm, shallow coastal waters and coral reefs of the Atlantic, Pacific, and Indian Oceans. Sadly, they are in danger because their nesting beaches are being destroyed and they accidentally get caught in fishing nets.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们在穿越大西洋、太平洋和印度洋温暖、浅水沿岸水域和珊瑚礁时滑翔。不幸的是，因为它们筑巢的海滩被破坏，并且偶尔会被渔网缠住，它们正处于危险之中。

habitat.text_en: Tropical and subtropical coastal waters, seagrass beds
habitat.text_zh: 热带和亚热带沿海水域，海草床

description.en: The saltwater crocodile is a massive, hypercarnivorous reptile and the largest living predator on land and in rivers.
description.zh: 湾鳄是一种巨大的超级肉食性爬行动物，也是陆地和河流中现存体型最大的捕食者。

anatomy.en: Meet the real monster of the rivers, also known as the 'saltie'! These are the largest living reptiles on Earth, with big males growing up to 20 feet (6 meters) long. They have thick, bumpy scales acting like tough armor, a massive tail for powerful swimming, and jaws that have the strongest bite force of any animal.
anatomy.zh: 来见见真正的河流怪兽，也被称为「咸水鳄」！它们是地球上现存最大的爬行动物，巨大的雄性可长达20英尺（6米）。它们有厚实凹凸不平的鳞片就像坚硬的盔甲，一条便于强力游泳的巨大尾巴，以及拥有所有动物中最强咬合力的下巴。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Salties are apex predators and master ninjas of the water. They are ambush hunters, waiting perfectly still under the murky water with just their eyes and nostrils sticking out. If a fish, bird, or even a large buffalo comes too close to the water's edge to drink, the crocodile strikes in a flash!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 咸水鳄是水中的顶级捕食者和忍者大师。它们是伏击猎手，一动不动地潜伏在浑浊的水下，只露出眼睛和鼻孔。如果鱼、鸟甚至巨大的水牛靠水边太近喝水，鳄鱼会在一瞬间发动攻击！

habitat_and_distribution.en: They are incredible swimmers and can be found in salty mangrove swamps, river mouths, and occasionally traveling far out into the ocean. Their homes stretch across Southeast Asia, from the eastern coast of India all the way down to northern Australia.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们是令人难以置信的游泳健将，出没在咸水红树林沼泽、河口，偶尔也会游到远洋中。它们的家园横跨东南亚，从印度东海岸一直向南延伸到澳大利亚北部。

habitat.text_en: Mangrove swamps, estuaries, and river mouths of the Indo-Pacific
habitat.text_zh: 印度-太平洋地区的红树林沼泽、河口及入海口

description.en: The Komodo dragon is the world's largest lizard species, endemic to a handful of Indonesian islands and known for its venomous bite.
description.zh: 科莫多巨蜥是世界上体型最大的蜥蜴物种，是印度尼西亚少数岛屿的特有物种，以其有毒的咬合而闻名。

anatomy.en: Looking at a Komodo dragon is like looking at a dinosaur! They are the heaviest and longest lizards in the world, growing up to 10 feet long. They are covered in tough, chain-mail-like scales. Their most famous feature is a long, yellow, forked tongue that constantly flicks in and out.
anatomy.zh: 看着科莫多巨蜥就像在看恐龙！它们是世界上最重、最长的蜥蜴，可以长到10英尺长。它们全身覆盖着坚韧的、像锁子甲一样的鳞片。它们最著名的特征是一条长长的、分叉的黄色舌头，不断地吐进吐出。

ecology_and_behavior.en: They don't breathe fire, but they do have venom! When they bite a large animal like a deer or water buffalo, the venom causes the animal to get weak over time. They use their forked tongue to 'taste' the air, helping them track down a meal from miles away. When they are babies, they live up in trees to avoid being eaten by bigger dragons.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们不会喷火，但它们确实有毒液！当它们咬伤鹿或水牛等大型动物时，毒液会让动物随着时间的推移变得虚弱。它们用分叉的舌头来「品尝」空气，帮助它们追踪几英里外的食物。在它们还是幼崽时，它们住在树上，以避免被更大的巨蜥吃掉。

habitat_and_distribution.en: In the entire world, wild Komodo dragons only live on a few hot, dry, volcanic islands in Indonesia, mostly inside Komodo National Park. Because their island home is so small, rising ocean waters from climate change are a huge threat to them.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 在全世界，野生的科莫多巨蜥只生活在印度尼西亚几个炎热、干燥的火山岛上，主要在科莫多国家公园内。因为它们的岛屿家园太小了，气候变化导致的海水上升对它们构成了巨大的威胁。

habitat.text_en: Savanna and volcanic forests of Komodo National Park, Indonesia
habitat.text_zh: 印度尼西亚科莫多国家公园的稀树草原与火山森林

description.en: The green iguana is a large, arboreal, mostly herbivorous lizard widely distributed across the Americas.
description.zh: 绿鬣蜥是一种大型的树栖蜥蜴，主要以草食为主，广泛分布于美洲大陆。

anatomy.en: Green iguanas are striking reptiles with a row of spikey, comb-like scales running all the way down their back to their long, whip-like tail. Under their chin, they have a large floppy piece of skin called a 'dewlap' that they puff out to say hello or look scary. Even though their name says 'green,' they can actually be bright orange, brown, or even blue!
anatomy.zh: 绿鬣蜥是引人注目的爬行动物，背部有一排如梳子般的尖锐鳞片，一直延伸到像鞭子一样的长尾巴。在它们的下巴下面，有一块巨大的、松垂的皮肤，叫做「喉扇」，它们会把喉扇鼓起来打招呼或让自己看起来很吓人。虽然它们的名字叫「绿」，但它们实际上也可以是亮橙色、棕色，甚至蓝色！

ecology_and_behavior.en: These big lizards are vegetarians, spending their lazy days high up in the treetops munching on juicy leaves, flowers, and fruit. They are excellent tree climbers. If a bird or snake tries to attack them, they will let go of the branch and drop straight down into a river, swimming away quickly like a little crocodile.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 这些大蜥蜴是素食主义者，它们慵懒的时光都在高高的树冠上度过，大口嚼着多汁的树叶、花朵和水果。它们是爬树高手。如果鸟或蛇试图攻击它们，它们会松开树枝直接掉进河里，像小鳄鱼一样迅速游走。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They love the heat and live in the tropical, rainy forests of Central and South America. They have also been brought by humans to places like Florida, where you can often see them sunbathing on warm concrete sidewalks.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们喜欢炎热，生活在中南美洲热带、多雨的森林里。它们也被人类带到了佛罗里达等地，在那里你经常可以看到它们在温暖的水泥人行道上晒太阳。

habitat.text_en: Canopies of tropical rainforests in Central and South America
habitat.text_zh: 中南美洲热带雨林的树冠层

description.en: The golden poison frog is a tiny endemic amphibian of Colombia, containing enough alkaloid toxin to kill multiple adult humans.
description.zh: 黄金箭毒蛙是哥伦比亚特有的一种微小两栖动物，其体内含有的生物碱毒素足以杀死多名成年人。

anatomy.en: Don't let its size fool you! This frog is only about two inches long, fitting perfectly in the palm of your hand, but it is super bright! It can be a dazzling mint green, yellow, or orange. This glowing color is nature's stop sign, warning hungry predators: 'Do not eat me!'
anatomy.zh: 别被它的体型骗了！这只青蛙只有大约两英寸长，完全可以放在你的手掌心里，但它颜色极其鲜艳！它可以是耀眼的薄荷绿、黄色或橙色。这种发光的颜色是大自然的停车标志，警告饥饿的捕食者：「别吃我！」

ecology_and_behavior.en: The golden poison frog is considered the most poisonous animal on Earth. A single tiny frog has enough poison in its skin to take down 10 grown adults! The amazing part is they don't make the poison themselves; they absorb it by eating special toxic ants and beetles in the jungle. They are also caring fathers, giving piggyback rides to their tadpoles.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 黄金箭毒蛙被认为是地球上最毒的动物。一只小小的青蛙皮肤里的毒素就足以放倒10个成年人！神奇的是，它们自己并不制造毒素；它们通过吃丛林里特殊的有毒蚂蚁和甲虫来吸收毒素。它们也是有爱的父亲，会背着它们的蝌蚪到处跑。

habitat_and_distribution.en: These deadly but beautiful frogs live in only one place in the whole world: a tiny patch of incredibly wet, humid rainforest on the Pacific coast of Colombia. Their home is disappearing as trees are cut down for farming.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 这些致命但美丽的青蛙只生活在全世界唯一的一个地方：哥伦比亚太平洋沿岸一小片极其潮湿、多雨的热带雨林中。由于人们为了种地砍伐树木，它们的家园正在消失。

habitat.text_en: Primary humid forests of the Chocó region in Colombia
habitat.text_zh: 哥伦比亚乔科地区的潮湿原始森林

description.en: The axolotl is a critically endangered paedomorphic salamander endemic to the lake complex of Xochimilco near Mexico City.
description.zh: 墨西哥钝口螈是一种极危的童态型蝾螈，特产于墨西哥城附近的霍奇米尔科湖区。

anatomy.en: The axolotl looks like a happy, underwater alien! It has a wide, cute face that looks like it's always smiling. On the sides of its head, it wears a fuzzy 'crown' of feathery gills that help it breathe. Because it never goes through metamorphosis like a regular frog or salamander, it stays looking like a baby forever.
anatomy.zh: 墨西哥钝口螈看起来像一个快乐的水下外星人！它有一张宽大、可爱的脸，看起来总是在微笑。在它的头两侧，戴着一顶毛茸茸的、羽毛状鳃组成的「王冠」，这能帮助它呼吸。因为它从不像普通的青蛙或蝾螈那样经历变态发育，所以它永远保持着婴儿般的样子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Axolotls have an incredible, real-life superpower: regeneration! If an axolotl hurts or loses an arm, a leg, its tail, or even parts of its heart and brain, it can completely grow them back without a single scar. They walk slowly on the muddy lake bottom, sucking up worms and small fish like an underwater vacuum cleaner.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 墨西哥钝口螈拥有一种令人难以置信的现实生活中的超能力：再生！如果墨西哥钝口螈受伤或失去了一条胳膊、一条腿、尾巴，甚至心脏和大脑的一部分，它可以完全重新长出来，而且不留一点疤痕。它们在泥泞的湖底缓慢行走，像水下吸尘器一样吸食蠕虫和小鱼。

habitat_and_distribution.en: In the wild, these amazing creatures only exist in one single place on Earth: the ancient, murky canals of Lake Xochimilco in Mexico City. Sadly, pollution and the sprawling city have made them critically endangered in nature.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 在野外，这些神奇的生物只存在于地球上的一个地方：墨西哥城霍奇米尔科湖古老、浑浊的运河中。令人悲伤的是，污染和不断扩张的城市让它们在自然界中处于极度濒危的状态。

habitat.text_en: Lake Xochimilco canal network, Mexico City
habitat.text_zh: 墨西哥城霍奇米尔科湖运河网络

description.en: The red kangaroo is the largest terrestrial mammal native to Australia, famous for its powerful hind legs and marsupial pouch.
description.zh: 红袋鼠是澳大利亚原产的最大陆生哺乳动物，以其强有力的后腿和有袋动物的育儿袋而闻名。

anatomy.en: Red kangaroos are the undisputed giants of Australia. Big males have reddish-brown fur and can stand over 6 feet tall, packing serious muscles. They have gigantic back feet made like biological springs for jumping, and a thick, muscular tail that is so strong it acts like a fifth leg when they walk slowly.
anatomy.zh: 红袋鼠是澳大利亚无可争议的巨人。高大的雄性长着红褐色的皮毛，站立时可超过6英尺高，浑身都是结实的肌肉。它们有巨大的后脚，就像生物弹簧一样适合跳跃，还有一条粗壮、肌肉发达的尾巴，力量大到在它们慢走时就像第五条腿一样。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Instead of running, they hop! A red kangaroo can leap up to 30 feet forward in a single bound, flying across the desert at 40 miles per hour. Mother kangaroos have a cozy pocket on their belly called a pouch. A tiny, jellybean-sized baby, called a joey, climbs inside and stays there for months until it's big enough to hop around.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们不跑，而是跳！一只红袋鼠一次跳跃就能向前跃出30英尺，以每小时40英里的速度飞跃沙漠。袋鼠妈妈的肚子上有一个舒适的口袋叫做育儿袋。一个像软糖豆那么小、被称为joey的宝宝会爬进去，在里面待上几个月，直到长得足够大可以跳来跳去为止。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They are the kings of the Outback. They roam the vast, sun-baked deserts and dry grasslands of central Australia, resting in the shade during the hottest parts of the day.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们是澳洲内陆之王。它们漫步在澳大利亚中部广阔、被太阳炙烤的沙漠和干旱的草原上，在一天中最热的时候躲在阴凉处休息。

habitat.text_en: Arid and semi-arid scrublands of the Australian Outback
habitat.text_zh: 澳大利亚内陆干旱及半干旱灌木丛带

description.en: The koala is an arboreal herbivorous marsupial native to Australia, subsisting almost entirely on a diet of eucalyptus leaves.
description.zh: 考拉是一种原产于澳大利亚的树栖食草有袋动物，几乎完全以桉树叶为食。

anatomy.en: Koalas look exactly like stuffed teddy bears come to life! They have fluffy round ears, a big leathery black nose, and thick gray fur. To help them live in trees, they have two thumbs on their front paws and razor-sharp curved claws that grip tightly onto bark.
anatomy.zh: 考拉看起来就像是活过来的毛绒泰迪熊！它们有毛茸茸的圆耳朵、大大的黑色皮革状鼻子和厚厚的灰色皮毛。为了帮助它们在树上生活，它们前爪上有两个拇指，还有像剃刀一样锋利的弯曲爪子，可以紧紧抓住树皮。

ecology_and_behavior.en: If there was an award for sleeping, koalas would win! They sleep up to 20 hours a day. Why? Because they only eat the leaves of eucalyptus trees. These leaves are actually toxic to most animals and don't provide a lot of energy, so koalas have to sleep a lot to digest them and save energy.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 如果有一个睡觉比赛，考拉一定会赢！它们每天能睡长达20个小时。为什么呢？因为它们只吃桉树的叶子。这些叶子实际上对大多数动物都有毒，而且不能提供很多能量，所以考拉必须睡很多觉来消化它们并保存能量。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Koalas live high up in the branches of eucalyptus forests along the eastern coast of Australia. Unfortunately, massive bushfires and human cities are destroying their forests, making them an endangered species.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 考拉高高地生活在澳大利亚东海岸桉树林的树枝上。不幸的是，大规模的丛林大火和人类城市正在摧毁它们的森林，使它们成为濒危物种。

habitat.text_en: Eucalyptus woodlands along Australia's eastern and southeastern coasts
habitat.text_zh: 澳大利亚东部和东南部沿海的桉树林

description.en: The giant anteater is a large insectivorous mammal native to Central and South America, characterized by its long tubular snout.
description.zh: 大食蚁兽是一种原产于中南美洲的大型食虫哺乳动物，以其长长的管状吻部为特征。

anatomy.en: The giant anteater is one of the strangest-looking animals around! It has a tiny head, no teeth at all, and a snout that looks like a long vacuum cleaner tube. It also has a massive, bushy tail that looks like a flag. When it sleeps, it curls up and uses its tail as a warm, camouflaged blanket.
anatomy.zh: 大食蚁兽是周围长相最奇怪的动物之一！它的头很小，完全没有牙齿，还有一个看起来像长吸尘器管子的吻部。它还有一条像旗帜一样巨大而蓬松的尾巴。当它睡觉时，它会蜷缩起来，把尾巴当成温暖的伪装毯子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Instead of teeth, it has a sticky, spaghetti-like tongue that is 2 feet long! It uses giant, sharp front claws to tear open tough termite mounds, then flicks its tongue in and out 150 times a minute. It can eat up to 30,000 ants and termites in a single day. Baby anteaters look like miniature adults and ride on their mom's back for almost a year.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它没有牙齿，取而代之的是一条长达2英尺的、像意大利面一样黏稠的舌头！它用巨大锋利的前爪撕开坚硬的白蚁丘，然后每分钟将舌头弹进弹出150次。它一天就能吃掉多达30,000只蚂蚁和白蚁。小食蚁兽看起来像缩小版的成年食蚁兽，会在妈妈的背上骑将近一年。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They roam across the grassy savannas and dense rainforests of Central and South America. Sadly, their homes are being taken over by farms and roads, putting them at risk.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们在中南美洲长满草的大草原和茂密的热带雨林中漫游。可悲的是，它们的家园正在被农场和公路占据，使它们面临危险。

habitat.text_en: Tropical and subtropical grasslands, savannas, and forests of South America
habitat.text_zh: 南美热带和亚热带草原、稀树草原和森林

description.en: The capybara is a giant cavy rodent native to South America. It is the largest living rodent in the world.
description.zh: 水豚是一种原产于南美的巨型豚鼠类啮齿动物。它是世界上最大的现存啮齿动物。

anatomy.en: Imagine a guinea pig, but make it the size of a large dog! That's a capybara. They have chunky, barrel-shaped bodies covered in coarse brown hair. Their eyes, ears, and nostrils are all grouped together at the very top of their square-shaped head, which lets them stay almost completely underwater while still breathing and looking around.
anatomy.zh: 想象一只豚鼠，但把它变成一只大狗那么大！那就是水豚。它们有着粗壮的桶状身体，覆盖着粗糙的棕色毛发。它们的眼睛、耳朵和鼻孔都聚集在方形脑袋的最顶端，这让它们能几乎完全潜入水下，同时还能呼吸和环顾四周。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Capybaras are famous for being incredibly chill and friendly. They live in large groups and spend a lot of time sitting in muddy water to cool down and escape predators like jaguars. They have webbed toes for swimming. Sometimes, other animals like birds, turtles, or even monkeys will sit on top of a capybara's head!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 水豚以难以置信的冷静和友好而闻名。它们群居生活，花很多时间坐在泥水里乘凉，躲避美洲豹等捕食者。它们长有带蹼的脚趾用来游泳。有时候，像鸟、乌龟甚至猴子等其他动物会坐在水豚的头顶上！

habitat_and_distribution.en: Water is a capybara's best friend. They live anywhere in South America where there are rivers, lakes, flooded grasslands, or swamps. They eat grass and aquatic plants, chewing constantly to keep their ever-growing front teeth filed down.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 水是水豚最好的朋友。在南美洲，只要有河流、湖泊、被淹没的草原或沼泽，它们就能住在那里。它们吃草和水生植物，不断咀嚼以把不断生长的门牙磨短。

habitat.text_en: Wetlands, flooded savannas, and riversides of South America
habitat.text_zh: 南美的湿地、泛滥平原及河岸地带

description.en: The largest living cat species, easily recognized by its dark vertical stripes on reddish-orange fur.
description.zh: 体型最大的现存猫科动物，以红橙色毛皮上的深色垂直条纹而闻名。

anatomy.en: The tiger is the biggest cat in the world, weighing up to 300 kilograms! They have beautiful orange fur with dark black stripes, and just like human fingerprints, no two tigers have the exact same stripe pattern.
anatomy.zh: 老虎是世界上最大的猫科动物，体重可达300公斤！它们拥有美丽的橙色毛皮和深黑色的条纹，就像人类的指纹一样，没有两只老虎的条纹是完全相同的。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Tigers are excellent swimmers and love to cool off in rivers and lakes. They hunt alone, mostly at night, using their stealth and powerful bodies to catch large prey like deer and wild boars.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 老虎是游泳健将，喜欢在河流和湖泊中乘凉。它们通常在夜间独自狩猎，利用出色的隐蔽能力和强壮的身体来捕捉鹿和野猪等大型猎物。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Tigers live in scattered forests and grasslands across Asia, from the freezing snowy forests of Russia to the hot tropical jungles of India and Indonesia. Sadly, they are very endangered because of habitat loss.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 老虎生活在亚洲各地零星的森林和草原中，从俄罗斯寒冷的雪林到印度和印度尼西亚炎热的热带丛林。遗憾的是，由于栖息地丧失，它们非常濒危。

habitat.text_en: Asian forests and grasslands
habitat.text_zh: 亚洲森林与草原

description.en: The largest cat in the Americas, known for its beautiful spotted coat and love of swimming.
description.zh: 美洲最大的猫科动物，以其美丽的斑点外衣和热爱游泳而闻名。

anatomy.en: Jaguars are stocky, powerful cats with gorgeous golden fur covered in black spots called rosettes. Each rosette has a smaller black spot inside it, which helps tell them apart from leopards!
anatomy.zh: 美洲豹是粗壮有力的猫科动物，金色的毛皮上布满了被称为玫瑰斑的黑色斑点。每个玫瑰斑里面还有一个更小的黑点，这能帮我们把它们和花豹区分开来！

ecology_and_behavior.en: Unlike most cats, jaguars absolutely love water! They are amazing swimmers and will even dive into rivers to hunt for fish, turtles, and even large crocodiles using their incredibly strong jaws.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 和大多数猫科动物不同，美洲豹绝对热爱水！它们是游泳高手，甚至会潜入河中，用极其强健的下颚捕食鱼类、海龟，甚至大型鳄鱼。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They rule the dense, wet tropical rainforests and swamps of Central and South America. The vast Amazon Rainforest is their main home, where they are the top predators.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们统治着中美洲和南美洲茂密潮湿的热带雨林和沼泽。广阔的亚马逊雨林是它们的主要家园，在那里它们是顶级捕食者。

habitat.text_en: Tropical rainforests of the Americas
habitat.text_zh: 美洲热带雨林

description.en: A highly adaptable and stealthy big cat famous for its spotted coat and tree-climbing skills.
description.zh: 一种适应性极强且隐秘的大型猫科动物，以斑点外衣和爬树技巧而闻名。

anatomy.en: Leopards are sleek and fast big cats with golden fur covered in dark, flower-shaped spots. Some leopards are born with completely black fur and are often called 'black panthers', but they still have hidden spots!
anatomy.zh: 豹子是流畅敏捷的大型猫科动物，金色的毛皮上长满深色的花朵状斑点。有些豹子出生时毛发全黑，常被称为「黑豹」，但它们身上其实隐藏着斑点！

ecology_and_behavior.en: They are the ultimate tree-climbing champions of the cat world. A leopard is so strong that it can carry a heavy meal up high into the branches of a tree to keep it safe from hungry lions and hyenas.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们是猫科动物中终极的爬树冠军。豹子非常强壮，能把沉重的猎物拖到高高的树枝上，以防被饥饿的狮子和鬣狗抢走。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Leopards are very adaptable and live in more places than any other wild cat. You can find them in African savannas, dry deserts, and even snowy mountains and forests in Asia.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 豹子的适应力很强，比其他任何野生猫科动物生活的地方都多。你可以在非洲的稀树草原、干旱的沙漠，甚至亚洲的雪山和森林中找到它们。

habitat.text_en: African and Asian diverse habitats
habitat.text_zh: 非洲和亚洲的多种栖息地

description.en: A beautiful, elusive big cat adapted to live in the freezing, high-altitude mountains of Central Asia.
description.zh: 一种美丽而难以捉摸的大型猫科动物，适应了中亚寒冷的高海拔山脉生活。

anatomy.en: Snow leopards have thick, smoky-gray fur with dark spots to blend in with rocky mountains. They have huge, furry paws that act like natural snowshoes, and an incredibly long, fluffy tail they wrap around themselves like a scarf to stay warm!
anatomy.zh: 雪豹拥有浓密的烟灰色毛皮和深色斑点，能与岩石山脉融为一体。它们有巨大的毛茸茸的爪子，就像天然的雪地靴，还有一条极长且蓬松的尾巴，可以像围巾一样裹住自己保暖！

ecology_and_behavior.en: Known as the 'ghosts of the mountains', they are very shy and rarely seen by humans. They can leap over 9 meters (30 feet) across freezing cliffs to catch wild sheep and goats.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们被称为「雪山之鬼」，非常害羞，极少被人类看到。它们能在冰冷的悬崖间跳跃超过9米（30英尺）来捕捉野生绵羊和山羊。

habitat_and_distribution.en: These majestic cats live high up in the cold, snowy mountain ranges of Central and South Asia, including the famous Himalayas. They stay far above the tree line where the air is very thin.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 这些雄伟的猫科动物生活在中亚和南亚寒冷的雪山之巅，包括著名的喜马拉雅山脉。它们停留在空气非常稀薄的树线之上。

habitat.text_en: High mountains of Central and South Asia
habitat.text_zh: 中南亚高山地区

description.en: A secretive forest cat from Southeast Asia, sporting large cloud-like spots and the longest canine teeth relative to body size of any living feline.
description.zh: 一种来自东南亚的隐秘森林猫，拥有云朵般的大斑点，以及现存猫科动物中相对于体型最长的犬齿。

anatomy.en: The clouded leopard gets its name from the beautiful, large cloud-shaped spots on its fur. It is a medium-sized cat, but it has the longest teeth compared to its body size of any wild cat today—just like a mini saber-toothed tiger!
anatomy.zh: 云豹因其皮毛上美丽的云状大斑点而得名。它是一种中型猫科动物，但拥有现存野生猫科动物中相对于体型最长的牙齿——就像一只迷你剑齿虎！

ecology_and_behavior.en: They are gymnasts of the jungle! Clouded leopards can hang upside down from branches by their large hind paws and even run headfirst down tree trunks like squirrels. They hunt birds, monkeys, and small deer.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们是丛林里的体操运动员！云豹可以用巨大的后爪倒挂在树枝上，甚至像松鼠一样头朝下从树干上跑下来。它们捕食鸟类、猴子和小型鹿类。

habitat_and_distribution.en: These elusive cats hide deep in the tropical rainforests and lush mountains of Southeast Asia, including parts of China, India, and Malaysia. They spend most of their lives high up in the dense tree canopy.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 这些难以捉摸的猫科动物隐藏在东南亚的热带雨林和郁郁葱葱的山脉深处，包括中国、印度和马来西亚的部分地区。它们一生中大部分时间都在茂密的树冠高处度过。

habitat.text_en: Tropical forests of Southeast Asia
habitat.text_zh: 东南亚热带森林

description.en: A medium-sized wildcat known for its incredible jumping ability and striking black-tufted ears.
description.zh: 一种中型野猫，以惊人的跳跃能力和醒目的黑色簇毛耳朵而闻名。

anatomy.en: The caracal is a slender, sandy-colored cat with a very special feature: long, pointy ears topped with tall black tufts of hair! These ear tufts look like little antennas and help them hear the faintest sounds in the wild.
anatomy.zh: 狞猫是一种纤细的沙色猫科动物，有一个非常特别的特征：尖尖的长耳朵顶端长着高高的黑色簇毛！这些耳簇看起来像小天线，帮助它们在野外听到最微弱的声音。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Caracals are high-jumping champions! They have super strong back legs that allow them to leap straight up into the air—up to 3 meters (10 feet) high—to snatch birds right out of the sky before they can fly away.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 狞猫是跳高冠军！它们拥有超级强壮的后腿，能让它们笔直地跳到空中——高达3米（10英尺）——在鸟儿飞走之前一把将它们从空中抓下。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They love dry places and are found in the woodlands, savannas, and scrub deserts of Africa, the Middle East, and parts of India. Their sandy fur perfectly camouflages them in the dry grass.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们喜欢干燥的地方，分布在非洲、中东和印度部分地区的林地、稀树草原和灌木沙漠中。它们沙色的皮毛能完美地隐蔽在干草中。

habitat.text_en: Dry savannas and woodlands of Africa and Asia
habitat.text_zh: 非洲和亚洲的干旱草原与林地

description.en: A slender, spotted African wildcat with the longest legs and largest ears relative to its body size.
description.zh: 一种身形修长、长满斑点的非洲野猫，拥有相对于体型最长的腿和最大的耳朵。

anatomy.en: The serval looks like a mini cheetah! It is a medium-sized cat with a beautiful golden coat covered in black spots. For its size, it has the longest legs and biggest ears of any cat in the world, making it look like a little supermodel.
anatomy.zh: 薮猫看起来就像迷你猎豹！它是一种中型猫科动物，披着带有黑斑的美丽金色外衣。就其体型而言，它是世界上所有猫科动物中腿最长、耳朵最大的，看起来像个小超模。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Those huge ears act like radar dishes, allowing the serval to hear mice moving under the ground. When it finds a mouse, it leaps high into the air and pounces down on it with both front paws. They are incredibly successful hunters!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 那些巨大的耳朵就像雷达天线，让薮猫能听到老鼠在地下移动的声音。当发现老鼠时，它会高高跃向空中，然后用两只前爪猛扑下去。它们是极其成功的猎手！

habitat_and_distribution.en: Servals live in the tall grasslands and wetlands of sub-Saharan Africa. They love areas near water where tall reeds and grass help hide them while they hunt for frogs, birds, and rodents.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 薮猫生活在撒哈拉以南非洲的高草草原和湿地。它们喜欢靠近水的地方，那里高高的芦苇和草丛有助于它们在捕食青蛙、鸟类和啮齿动物时隐藏自己。

habitat.text_en: African savannas and wetlands
habitat.text_zh: 非洲稀树草原与湿地

description.en: A beautiful, medium-sized wildcat with striking leopard-like spots, found in the forests of the Americas.
description.zh: 一种美丽的中型野猫，身上带有醒目的类似豹子的斑点，分布在美洲的森林中。

anatomy.en: The ocelot is about twice the size of a normal pet cat. It is famous for its breathtaking fur, which is covered in chain-like dark spots and stripes. They have bright, large eyes that give them excellent night vision for hunting in the dark.
anatomy.zh: 虎猫大约是普通宠物猫的两倍大。它以令人惊叹的皮毛而闻名，上面布满了链状的深色斑点和条纹。它们拥有明亮的大眼睛，在黑暗中狩猎时有着极好的夜视能力。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Ocelots are solitary, nocturnal hunters. They roam the forest floor looking for monkeys, birds, and iguanas, but they are also great swimmers and climbers. They use their raspy tongues to clean every bit of meat off their bones.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 虎猫是独居的夜间猎手。它们在森林地面上漫游，寻找猴子、鸟类和鬣蜥，同时也是游泳和爬树好手。它们用粗糙的舌头把骨头上的每一丁点肉都舔得干干净净。
